subject: Adding VES 5.4.1 Event Listener Spec
commit_message:
Adding VES 5.4.1 Event Listener Spec

The ONAP Frankfurt release still supports
the VES 5.4.1 Event Listener format so
this has been added back into the
requirements, and R-570134 has been
updated accordingly.

Issue-ID: VNFRQTS-804
Signed-off-by: Lovett, Trevor <trevor.lovett@att.com>
Change-Id: I24fb9d62314cc4e35c87b23ce57498363d2eb0f3

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/rtdv3-global-verify-master/437/ (1/2)

Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/doc-master-verify-rtd/6523/ (2/2)

Patch Set 1:

All - I am adding the VES 5.4.1 Event Listener Spec as we have determined that DCAE still supports it.  I have not made any changes to the content other that formating the line breaks and fixing some formatting that was causing some content not to be displayed.  This was copied from its prior location in VNFSDK from the Casablanca release.

I am not planning to entertain any content changes to the spec itself.

I've also updated R-570134 to denote that 5.4.1 is available, but 7.1.1 is preferred.

Please let me know if you have any issues or concerns.
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1
> 
> than

Marge - Is this "than" intended as feedback on something that needs to change?

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

Throttling was introduced under old VES spec (<4.0) but was not implemented due to conflicting design proposals. The initial design was to have VESCollector/DCAE trigger throttling via response (as documented in rst here), however the direction later changed to have this managed through controllers (eg. app-c/sdnc) and not manage under VES scope.

Would suggest removing throttling api references from documentation

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 2:

All - 

Based on Vijay's comments, I think we should remove the unsupported features from the specification. We're in a rather awkward position with this as it's been published for quite some time, but I still think it's better to have accurate information in the spec than leave it or introduce a new version. So my plan is to remove/amend the content Vijay has pointed to even thought that's an odd action to take with an old baselined spec. Please let me know if anyone disagrees with this course of action.

So it's easier to track the changes I am making, we're going to merge this change in and I'll deliver the removals in the Frankfurt release via https://jira.onap.org/browse/VNFRQTS-861. I think this is better than removing sections in a ticket that was created to simply copy it forward.

Thanks,
Trevor

Patch Set 2:

I agree with Trevor`s proposal.
If we don`t have an implementation, let`s not write about that.
